concerns.text: Task T021 (Normalization) is listed before T022 (Interaction Generation). The spec (FR-002) and standard ML practice require generating interaction features (e.g., Temp × Mg) on the raw numeric values BEFORE normalization. Normalizing first destroys the multiplicative relationship required for the interaction term. T021 must precede T022.

Task T031 (Random Forest training) is listed before T032 (Timeout mechanism). The timeout mechanism (FR-004) must wrap the training process. T032 (implementing the wrapper) must precede T031 (the actual training call) to ensure the timeout logic is in place before execution. Currently, the order implies training happens before the timeout is implemented.

Task T035 (Threshold sweep logic) is listed before T036 (Stability score calculation). The stability score (SC-002) is a derived metric calculated FROM the results of the threshold sweep. T036 cannot be implemented or executed until T035 has generated the sweep data. T035 must precede T036.

Task T037 (Confounder analysis logic) is listed before T038 (Report generation). T038 explicitly depends on the output of T037 (checking for proxies, comparing R²). While often in the same file, the logical dependency requires the analysis logic to be defined/ready before the report generation logic can consume it. T037 must precede T038.

Test tasks T010 and T011 are marked [P] (parallel-safe). However, they both target `tests/unit/test_download.py`. While pytest can run tests in parallel, they share the same module file. If the implementation of `download.py` is not yet complete (which it isn't, as T012+ are later), these tests will fail. The [P] tag is technically correct for execution order, but the dependency on the *implementation* tasks (T012+) is implicit. Ensure the runner knows T012+ must be implemented before T010/11 can pass, even if T010/11 are written first.
